wait:db file sequential read: High single-block IO latency. Check index access patterns, storage latency, and verify that hot blocks fit in buffer cache. Consider adding/adjusting indexes or reducing row-by-row lookups.
wait:direct path read: Direct path reads indicate large scans. Consider partition pruning, proper parallelism, and verifying that large full scans are expected for the workload.
wait:enq: TX - row lock contention: Row lock contention detected. Investigate conflicting DML transactions, commit frequency, and application logic. Consider shorter transactions and proper indexing to reduce hot spots.
wait:buffer busy waits: Buffer contention on hot blocks. Consider spreading workload (hash partitioning), increasing INITRANS on hot segments, or addressing hot block access patterns.
wait:log file sync: Commit latency is high. Check redo log device latency and size, verify low commit batching, and consider tuning filesystem or storage for sequential writes.
wait:log file parallel write: Redo writes are delayed. Review I/O bandwidth, RAID cache, and synchronous write settings for redo logs. Consider separate redo disk group.
wait:latch free: Latch contention observed. Check shared pool sizing, hot latch locations, and excessive library cache pinning.
wait:*: General wait event. Review top SQLs and segments contributing around the chosen snaps. Correlate with IO, concurrency, or commit hotspots.
sql:high_cpu: High CPU-consuming SQL detected. Review join methods, missing indexes, and plan stability. Consider using SQL Profiles or re-gathering stats if misestimation is observed.
sql:long_elapsed: SQL shows long elapsed time. Check parallelism, data skew, and optimizer mode. Consider breaking the workload or using materialized results.
sql:*: Problematic SQL. Validate plan stability, predicates, and join methods. Confirm proper indexing, up-to-date stats, and consider SQL profile/outline only after root cause analysis.
segment:undo$: Undo tablespace shows contention. Review undo tablespace sizing and concurrent transaction behavior.
segment:*: Hot segment detected. Validate access patterns, clustering, and partitioning. Consider targeted indexing, segment reorg, or ILM to reduce skew and hotspots.
